2606.10968.title: Beyond Uniform Token-Level Trust Region in LLM Reinforcement Learning
2606.10968.paper_summary: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has become standard for improving LLM reasoning. However, existing PPO-style trust-region mechanisms remain position-agnostic by enforcing uniform thresholds across all tokens independently. This pointwise treatment conflicts with autoregressive generation in two critical ways. First, uniform thresholds ignore autoregressive asymmetry. Early-stage deviations produce compounding sequence-level drift, causing static thresholds to under-regulate early divergence and excessively constrain late-stage exploration. Second, evaluating token-level divergence in isolation overlooks cumulative prefix drift, granting the same divergence allowance regardless of how far the conditioning history has already deviated from the rollout policy. To address this limitation, we propose CPPO (Cumulative Prefix-divergence Policy Optimization), a token-level masking rule that aligns updates with a finite-horizon policy-improvement bound via two coupled mechanisms. First, a position-weighted threshold imposes stricter limits at early positions whose effects persist longer, relaxing constraints for late-stage tokens. Second, a cumulative prefix budget tracks historical deviations, dynamically restricting further token-level deviation to prevent compounding errors along the prefix. Empirically, CPPO enhances training stability and significantly improves reasoning accuracy across various model scales.
2606.10968.paper_summary_zh: 強化學習與可驗證獎勵 (RLVR) 已成為提升大型語言模型 (LLM) 推理的標準。然而，現有的 PPO 風格信任區域機制仍然是位置無關的，因為它們獨立地對所有標記施加統一的閾值。這種逐點處理在兩個關鍵方面與自回歸生成相衝突。首先，統一的閾值忽略了自回歸的不對稱性。早期的偏差會產生累積的序列級漂移，導致靜態閾值對早期的偏離調控不足，並過度限制晚期的探索。其次，孤立地評估標記級的偏差忽視了累積的前綴漂移，無論條件歷史已經從展開策略偏離多遠，都賦予相同的偏差允許。為了解決這一限制，我們提出了 CPPO (累積前綴偏差政策優化)，這是一種標記級的遮罩規則，通過兩個耦合機制使更新與有限範圍的政策改進界限對齊。首先，位置加權閾值在早期位置施加更嚴格的限制，其效果持續時間更長，並為晚期標記放寬約束。其次，累積前綴預算追蹤歷史偏差，動態限制進一步的標記級偏差，以防止沿前綴的累積錯誤。實證結果顯示，CPPO 增強了訓練穩定性，並在各種模型規模上顯著提高了推理準確性。
2606.10968.author: Renjie Mao et.al.
2606.10968.authors: Renjie Mao, Xiangxin Zhou, Lvfang Tao, Yixin Ding, Yu Shi, Yongguang Lin, Yuheng Wu, Honglin Zhu, Qian Qiu, Wenxi Zhu
